surface.name: NVIDIA Developer, with the current CUDA Toolkit Ubuntu Debian Installer route retained as an unresolved candidate
surface.selection_basis: NVIDIA Developer presents simulation, accelerated computing, AI, cloud, robotics, embedded, and graphics surfaces as peers. CUDA Toolkit is retained as one bounded candidate surface because its official Quick Start explicitly promises installation plus verification that a CUDA application can run, but the current official route is not version-consistent enough to reconstruct end to end.

alternatives_considered.name: NVIDIA API Catalog
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer cloud and API route with account and API-key onboarding rather than the local accelerated-computing journey.

alternatives_considered.name: NVIDIA Omniverse
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer simulation and OpenUSD developer platform with different hardware, installation, and first-project paths.

alternatives_considered.name: NVIDIA NGC
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a peer catalog and cloud distribution surface with different account and artifact workflows.

entry_point.developer_intent: Choose an NVIDIA developer platform and reach the first success that its official getting-started material defines.
assumed_prior_state: No NVIDIA product choice, CUDA repository, CUDA Toolkit installation, environment configuration, sample checkout, sample build, or successful CUDA program is assumed.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: For the selected CUDA candidate, install CUDA and verify that a CUDA application can run.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The intended CUDA terminal is a successful nbody run on a supported Ubuntu system with a compatible NVIDIA GPU, but the current official installation and sample pages do not form one version-consistent executable path.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The intended signal is the built nbody CUDA sample running from its executable directory; the current guide does not publish one resolvable command and version-aligned location.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: install CUDA and verify that a CUDA application can run

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The current CUDA documentation defines the Quick Start as minimal installation and verification, and the Linux section names nbody as the verification application. The current route cannot be claimed complete because the CUDA 13.3 page publishes CUDA 12.8 environment paths, links to an obsolete sample directory, and the current nbody source adds dependencies not enumerated by the Quick Start.

prerequisites.requirement: Use a system with a CUDA-capable NVIDIA GPU that is supported by the selected CUDA Toolkit and driver combination.
Use a clean installation of a supported Ubuntu release for the selected Debian Installer candidate.
Have sudo access to install repository metadata, packages, and any required build tooling, then reboot the system.
Have network access to NVIDIA's CUDA repositories and the official cuda-samples repository.
Have Git, a supported host compiler, build tools, and CMake 3.20 or newer for the current official sample repository.
The current official nbody sample lists X11, OpenGL, Freeglut, and GLEW as build or run dependencies.

primary_path.action: Open NVIDIA Developer and choose the product or platform whose first mile is being measured.
details: The site presents simulation, accelerated computing, AI, cloud, robotics, embedded, and graphics routes as peers.
primary_path.output: Product and topic entry points are visible.
primary_path.success_signal: A platform-specific Get Started or documentation route can be selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The site does not name one company-wide developer first mile.

primary_path.action: For the retained candidate, choose CUDA Toolkit and open its Quick Start Guide.
details: The Quick Start branches by operating system, Linux distribution, architecture, and installer type.
primary_path.input: An NVIDIA product choice and target system
primary_path.success_signal: The Ubuntu Debian Installer path is selected for this candidate.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A different NVIDIA product, operating system, distribution, architecture, or installer changes the complete route.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The current CUDA 13.3 Quick Start publishes `/usr/local/cuda-12.8` environment paths and links to `Samples/5_Domain_Specific/nbody`, which is absent on the current official repository. The current repository uses `cpp/5_Domain_Specific/nbody`, targets CUDA 13.3, and lists additional X11, OpenGL, Freeglut, and GLEW dependencies. The public pages do not select a version-aligned reconciliation.
candidate_path_gap.impact: A developer cannot follow one exact official sequence from current CUDA installation through a resolvable nbody executable without choosing corrections or a release-aligned sample revision that the current Quick Start does not specify.

branches.condition: A different NVIDIA platform is intended
branches.path: Use that platform's own Get Started journey.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the entire prerequisite, account, installation, and success model.

branches.condition: The target is Windows or another supported Linux distribution
branches.path: Use the operating-system and distribution branch published by the CUDA Quick Start.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the installer, package commands, environment configuration, and sample build steps.

branches.condition: A Runfile, Conda, or different repository installer is preferred
branches.path: Use that installer-specific route instead of the Ubuntu Debian Installer candidate.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the acquisition and installation chronology before sample verification.

branches.condition: A release-aligned sample archive is selected instead of current master
branches.path: Obtain the version-specific writable sample bundle linked for the installed toolkit.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Can change sample directory names, build commands, and compatibility with the toolkit version.

friction_gates.description: NVIDIA Developer exposes many peer platforms without one company-wide first-success route.
The selected route requires a CUDA-capable NVIDIA GPU compatible with the toolkit and driver.
The CUDA route branches by operating system, distribution, architecture, installer type, and version.
Inside the selected CUDA candidate, the official Debian Installer path requires a system reboot before environment setup and sample verification.
Inside the selected CUDA candidate, the CUDA 13.1 Quick Start publishes CUDA 12.8 environment paths, creating an official version conflict that cannot be safely normalized without judgment.
Inside the selected CUDA candidate, the current sample repository adds a CMake 3.20 or newer build requirement and can diverge from the archived Quick Start's sample layout.

sources.title: Platforms and Tools | NVIDIA Developer
evidence_supported: Alternative simulation, AI, cloud, and accelerated-computing surfaces

sources.title: CUDA Toolkit - Free Tools and Training
CUDA Toolkit Documentation
evidence_supported: Quick Start as the minimal installation and verification route

uncertainties.question: Which NVIDIA platform should define a company-wide developer first mile?
uncertainties.impact: CUDA, API Catalog, Omniverse, NGC, Jetson, and other choices have unrelated access, hardware, setup, and success paths.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official NVIDIA Developer surfaces present them as peer platforms and topics rather than declaring one universal default.

uncertainties.question: Which exact CUDA Toolkit environment path and cuda-samples revision form one version-consistent candidate today?
uncertainties.impact: The published filesystem path, source layout, dependencies, build result, and runnable nbody location differ by answer.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The current documentation hub and sample repository target CUDA 13.3, while the current Quick Start publishes CUDA 12.8 environment paths and an obsolete nbody directory. The current nbody page also adds dependencies absent from the Quick Start.

excluded_after_success: CUDA application authoring, debugging, profiling, optimization, deployment, and running the full sample test suite occur after nbody first runs.
NVIDIA account creation and cloud API-key flows belong to other NVIDIA products and are not imported into the local CUDA candidate.
